🏔️ Lo Barnechea, Santiago

Our home for eight days

6 floors · mountain views · all the good stuff

We've got a seriously stunning estate to ourselves — right at the foot of the Andes, 40 minutes from the airport, in one of Santiago's most exclusive neighbourhoods. Think mountain views, a heated pool, two jacuzzis, a cinema room, a gym, a Hobbit sauna, and a rooftop made for sunset drinks. Not bad, huh? 😎

☀️ Late Summer in the Andes

So much to do

You will not be bored. Promise.

March is peak late summer in Santiago — long warm days, dry skies, and the Andes in full colour. From vineyard day trips to canyon hikes to river rafting, there's something for every kind of Wong.

🎿 Adventures

Activities near the house

📍🥾 Cascada de las Ánimas
A stunning nature reserve with waterfalls, hiking trails, zip-lining, and horseback riding in a beautiful Andean canyon. A must-do.
Nature Reserve
📍⛳ Championship Golf
El Golf de Manquehue is basically on our doorstep. Andes as your backdrop, sunny March weather. Golfers: you're very welcome.
On-site Access
📍♨️ Hot Springs
Soak in natural geothermal hot springs deep in Cajón del Maipo. Perfect wind-down after a big day on the trails. Pure bliss.
Cajón del Maipo
📍🕌 Templo Bahá'í de Sudamérica
One of only eight Bahá'í temples in the world — and arguably the most architecturally stunning. Designed to look like a flower unfolding, it sits high in the foothills with sweeping views over Santiago. The reflection pool at dusk is something else entirely. Free entry, open to all faiths.
Lo Barnechea · 15 min

🏛️ Day Trips

Must-see Santiago

40 minutes to one of South America's coolest cities
📍⛪ Plaza de Armas
Santiago's historic heart — the stunning Metropolitan Cathedral, street performers, local coffee, and the buzz of a proper Latin American city square.
Historic Centre
📍⛰️ Cerro San Cristóbal
Take the funicular to the top for 360° views of Santiago with the full Andes wall behind it. Best photo of the trip, guaranteed.
Best Views
📍🎨 Barrio Bellavista
Santiago's coolest neighbourhood — murals everywhere, great restaurants, bars, and Pablo Neruda's famous house La Chascona. Super walkable.
Culture + Food

🌟 Family Picks

Added by the Wongs

🍷 Let's eat!

A foodie family's dream city

These are places we'd actually go — no filler

Santiago has quietly become one of South America's best food cities — and it's still flying under the radar internationally, which means world-class cooking without the price tags of LA or New York. Below are genuinely great restaurants, from a globally ranked tasting menu to a classic bistro packed every night for 30 years.

💸 Your money goes further here

World-class food. Half the price.

The Chilean peso means your dining dollar stretches in a way it simply doesn't at home. This isn't budget travel — it's eating at the best restaurants in South America and spending what you'd drop on a mid-tier Thursday night in Silver Lake or the West Village.

Tasting Menu Comparison
$85
Boragó
Santiago
$295
Equivalent
NYC
$275
Equivalent
LA
Bottle of Excellent Carménère
$18
Restaurant
Santiago

Restaurant

$65
Restaurant
LA
The bottom line
  • —A $$$$ tasting menu at Boragó runs ~$80–110 USD per person. In NYC that experience is $275+.
  • —A $$$ night out — great food, wine, cocktails — lands around $40–60pp. Half what you'd pay at Bestia or Bavel.
  • —Chilean wine by the bottle at a restaurant is laughably cheap. Order liberally. No guilt.
  • —The $$ spots (Liguria, Bocanáriz) are proper, full, satisfying meals for $20–30pp. Eat at these on repeat.

⭐ Special Occasion

When you want to go all out

🏆 #1 in Chile · Latin America's 50 Best
📍Boragó
Chef Rodolfo Guzmán is basically a culinary mad scientist — his seasonal tasting menu uses foraged, farmed, and fished-from-scratch Chilean ingredients you've never heard of. Sea algae, picorocos, nine-hour lamb. It's cerebral, delicious, and unforgettable. Plan 3+ hours. Book months ahead.
$$$$ · Barrio Jardín del Este · Closed Sun & Mon
Modern Chilean · Latin America's 50 Best
📍99 Restaurante
Think of it as Boragó's cooler, more relaxed younger sibling. Chef Kurt Schmidt (trained at Noma) crafts playful seasonal tasting menus around Chilean produce — innovative but approachable. Great for the group that wants fine dining without the stiff formality.
$$$ · Providencia · Book ahead
Creative · Playful · 50Best Discovery
📍Demencia
Fine dining with its hair down — neon lights, circus energy, and seriously good food. Chef Benjamín Nast blends Chilean ingredients with global flavours in unexpected ways. The oysters (4 different profiles) are the opener; the grilled octopus is legendary.
$$$ · Las Condes

🍽️ Great Any Night

Reliable, delicious, fun

Classic Chilean Bistro · Providencia
📍Liguria
The most quintessentially Santiago restaurant there is — packed every night for 30+ years. Traditional Chilean dishes (lamb shank, carne mechada, sea urchin), brilliant pisco cocktails, nostalgic décor. No reservations, so get there early and enjoy the wait.
$$ · Providencia · Multiple locations · No reservations
Wine Bar · Lastarria
📍Bocanáriz
The wine list here is basically a love letter to every Chilean region — hundreds of bottles covering the whole country. The food is excellent too. Sit in the gorgeous Lastarria neighbourhood, drink well, eat well. Walk-ins work if you're patient.
$$ · Barrio Lastarria · Walk-ins welcome
Nikkei (Japanese-Peruvian) · W Hotel
📍Karai by Mitsuharu
From the team behind Lima's legendary Maido — Japanese precision meets Chilean and Peruvian ingredients. The loco tiradito (Chile's prized mollusk with marine collagen mayo) is one of those bites you'll still be thinking about months later. Sleek, buzzy, delicious.
$$$ · Las Condes · Book ahead
Indigenous Chilean · Bellavista
📍Peumayén Ancestral Food
A genuine one-of-a-kind: tasting menus built entirely from ancestral Mapuche, Quechua, and Huilliche recipes and native ingredients — chochoca, llama, seaweed, mussels. The kind of meal that teaches you something about a country. Deeply moving and delicious.
$$$ · Bellavista · Book ahead

🇨🇱 Eat Like a Local

Chilean food cheat sheet

🍽️ Things to eat & drink while you're here

  • 🍹Pisco Sour — Chile's national cocktail. Pisco + lemon + sugar + egg white. Order one immediately upon arrival. Non-negotiable.
  • 🥟Empanadas de pino — beef, olive & boiled egg in a crispy pastry. Grab a bag from a local bakery. Absolute must.
  • 🍷Carménère wine — Chile's signature grape, and it's delicious. Pick up bottles at Maipo Valley to bring home.
  • 🦔Erizos (sea urchin) — fresh, creamy, extraordinary. Order it at Liguria. You'll understand why Chile is obsessed.
  • 🥩Asado — Chilean BBQ is serious business. Slow-cooked meats over wood fire. Get the full experience in Cajón del Maipo.
  • 🍲Cazuela — hearty meat and veggie stew. Perfect for a cool mountain evening back at the house.
  • 💡Tipping: 10% in restaurants is the norm. Cash is appreciated at local spots.

📋 The Essentials

Everything you need to know

Read this before you get on the plane 😄
✈️
Getting There
Fly into Santiago Airport (SCL) — Arturo Merino Benítez International. Well-connected from most major cities.

The house is ~40 min by car. We'll be renting cars — pick yours up straight from the airport and follow the crew to the house.

🚗 Rental desks at SCL — book ahead:

Chilean Rent a Car ↗
Local favourite · desks in T1 & T2 · strong reputation

Flexways ↗
Chilean company · competitive rates · SUVs available

Europcar Chile ↗
In-terminal desk · wide fleet · easy online booking

Sixt Chile ↗
Premium fleet · WhatsApp support · after-hours drop-off

Hertz Chile ↗
In-terminal · reliable · good for larger vehicles

💡 Book before you leave home — rates are significantly cheaper than walk-up. An automatic SUV fits 4–5 comfortably with luggage.

📍 Property Address:
2731 Sendero Las Lagunas ↗
Lo Barnechea, Santiago Metropolitan 7690000
Chile
📅
Dates & Times
Arrive: Sunday 1 March 2027
Leave: Sunday 8 March 2027

Check-in from 4:00 PM. Arriving early? Let us know and we'll arrange it with the concierge.
Check-out by 11:00 AM.
🌤️
Weather in March
March is late summer — long sunny days, zero rain, brilliant blue skies with the Andes in full view.

Lo Barnechea sits at ~850m elevation, about 5–7°F cooler than central Santiago at all times.

🌞 Daytime avg: 75–79°F
Warm, dry, and sunny. T-shirts and sunscreen weather.

🌙 Nighttime avg: 52–55°F
The Andes drop temperatures fast after sunset. A real jacket — not just a layer — is needed for evenings outdoors by the pool or on the rooftop.

☀️ UV Index: Very High (8–10)
High altitude + late summer sun = sunscreen is non-negotiable, especially on hikes.
💵
Money
Currency: Chilean Peso (CLP)

ATMs everywhere. Cards widely accepted. Good to have some cash for markets, tips, and smaller spots.

Tip: 10% in restaurants is standard.
🗣️
Language
Spanish is the language. Chilean Spanish is fast — locals love any effort you make!

Survival phrases:
Hola — Hello
Gracias — Thank you
La cuenta, por favor — The bill
Una pisco sour, por favor — Essential
🔒
Safety
The estate is in a secure gated community with 24/7 guards. Lo Barnechea is one of Santiago's safest areas.

Normal city awareness in busy spots like markets or public squares — keep valuables tucked away.
🏥
Health & Emergencies
Emergency numbers:
🚑 Ambulance: 131
👮 Police: 133
🚒 Fire: 132

World-class hospitals nearby: Clínica Las Condes ↗ and Clínica Alemana ↗. Get travel insurance with medical cover!
📶
WiFi & Mobile
The estate has fast WiFi throughout all 6 levels — no fighting over signal.

For mobile data, grab a local SIM from Entel or Movistar at the airport. International roaming works fine across Santiago too.
🚗
Getting Around by Car
A car is essential for Cajón del Maipo, the Maipo Valley wineries, and getting between Santiago neighbourhoods on your own schedule. We'll have cars — coordinate with the group on sharing rides.

All desks are inside SCL terminals — follow "Rent a Car" signs after baggage claim. No shuttles needed for most agencies.

Tips:
✦ Book online before you fly — 30–40% cheaper
✦ Get full CDW insurance — standard US/AU policies don't cover Chile
✦ Automatic SUVs are best for the mountain roads to Cajón del Maipo
✦ Google Maps works excellently throughout the region

🧳 What to Pack

Pack smart for the range

The ~25°F day-to-night swing catches everyone off guard
👕
Daytime (Pool, Terrace, City)
T-shirts & light tops — it's genuinely hot midday
Shorts & sundresses — pool and terrace days
Swimwear — the pool is heated, you will be in it
Comfortable walking shoes — cobblestones in Santiago
Sandals — for in and around the estate
Sunglasses — the Andean light is intense
🌙
Evenings (Dinners, Rooftop, Fire Pit)
A real jacket or light coat — not optional when temps drop to the low 50s°F
1–2 smart-casual outfits — Santiago restaurants dress up a notch
Light trousers or jeans — evenings cool fast
Layers you can peel — afternoon warmth lingers into dinner, then drops sharply
Closed shoes for nights out — restaurants and Bellavista bars
🥾
Activities (Hiking, Cajón del Maipo)
Hiking shoes or trail sneakers — Cascada de las Ánimas has uneven terrain
Sun hat or cap — UV index hits 8–10 at altitude
High-SPF sunscreen — reapply constantly up in the mountains
Daypack + water bottle — hydration matters at elevation
Light windbreaker — canyon trails can be breezy
Lip balm with SPF — dry Andean air is brutal
💊
Don't Forget
Travel insurance docs — required, not optional
Any prescription meds — bring more than you think you need
Power adapter — Chile uses Type C/L (230V)
Portable charger — for long day trips away from the house
Laundry machines on-site — pack lighter than you think
Cash (CLP) — markets and tips; get some at the airport ATM

🏠 House Rules & Good-to-Knows

  • The estate sleeps 16 max — our 14 have loads of room. Spread out and enjoy it!
  • Concierge is available 24/7 for restaurants, taxis, and activities. Use them for everything.
  • Laundry machines are on-site — pack light.
  • The pool and both jacuzzis are heated — evening swims are absolutely happening.
  • The home theatre seats 8 — movie nights may need two sessions. Or just get cozy 😄
  • Quiet hours after 11 PM in the gated community.
  • Smoking outdoors only — the rooftop and garden are perfect for it.
  • The house has a Hobbit-inspired sauna. Yes, really. Yes, you have to try it.
